original_hypothesis.title: BlackClaims recoverERC20 Can Drain Active Season Reward Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: BlackClaims.recoverERC20() allows the owner to withdraw ANY ERC20 token including the reward token (BLACK) during an active claiming season. There is no restriction preventing withdrawal of the reward token while remaining_reward_amount > 0. This would cause subsequent claimAndStakeReward calls to revert due to insufficient balance, permanently denying users their seasonal rewards.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The recoverERC20 function transfers the entire balance of any token to the owner without checking if it's the active reward token. This creates a single-function path to drain all season rewards.

proof_of_signal.pattern: recoverERC20(tokenAddress_) transfers full balance without checking if tokenAddress_ == address(token) or if season.remaining_reward_amount > 0

preconditions: Owner or secondOwner calls recoverERC20 with the reward token address

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: recoverERC20(address(token)) → transfers all BLACK balance → claimAndStakeReward → token.transfer fails → users cannot claim
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time during active claiming season

operational_questions: Who are the current owner and secondOwner addresses?
Is there an active season with unclaimed rewards?
Are there operational procedures preventing accidental recoverERC20 of reward token?
validation_requirements: Verify recoverERC20 has no reward token exclusion
Test claimAndStakeReward after recoverERC20 drains balance

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Compromised or malicious owner calls recoverERC20(address(token))
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All reward tokens transferred to owner address
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: All subsequent claims revert — users permanently lose rewards
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires owner key compromise or malicious admin

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All unclaimed seasonal rewards permanently lost to users
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Owner could return tokens manually, but no on-chain enforcement

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: recoverERC20 calls with reward token address
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Claim failures after admin token movements
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Owner withdrawing reward tokens during active season

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
